evals.prompt: We're migrating off our current change management tool and need to set up GitHub Actions for our Salesforce project. We have a dev hub, three sandboxes (dev, qa, staging), and production. Can you help us get started with CI/CD? We want to validate changes on PRs and deploy to production when we merge to main.
evals.expected_output: Complete GitHub Actions workflows including validate-pr.yml and deploy-production.yml, with authentication setup, testing strategy, and documentation on how to configure GitHub secrets.

assertions.name: Creates validate-pr workflow file
assertions.description: Should create a .github/workflows file for PR validation

assertions.name: Creates deploy-production workflow file
assertions.description: Should create a .github/workflows file for production deployment

assertions.name: Includes SF CLI installation
assertions.description: Workflows should install Salesforce CLI

assertions.name: Includes authentication steps
assertions.description: Workflows should authenticate to Salesforce orgs

assertions.name: Includes test execution
assertions.description: Workflows should run Apex tests

assertions.name: Provides setup documentation
assertions.description: Should include documentation on how to configure GitHub secrets

assertions.name: Explains branch strategy
assertions.description: Documentation should explain the branch workflow (PR → main → deploy)

evals.prompt: I need to test my Apex changes across three different Salesforce orgs simultaneously - our Financial Services Cloud sandbox, Health Cloud sandbox, and standard Enterprise Edition sandbox. Right now I have to manually deploy to each one which takes forever. How can I automate this with GitHub Actions?
evals.expected_output: GitHub Actions workflow using matrix strategy to run parallel deployments and tests across all three org types, with explanation of the architectural value and time savings.

assertions.name: Uses GitHub Actions matrix strategy
assertions.description: Workflow should include strategy.matrix configuration

assertions.name: Defines all three org types in matrix
assertions.description: Matrix should include FSC, Health Cloud, and Enterprise Edition

assertions.name: Deploys to all orgs in parallel
assertions.description: Workflow should trigger parallel jobs for each org

assertions.name: Explains time savings
assertions.description: Documentation should explain parallel vs sequential deployment time benefits

assertions.name: Includes authentication for multiple orgs
assertions.description: Should explain how to set up secrets for each org

assertions.name: References Pattern 1 from skill
assertions.description: Should demonstrate the Matrix Strategy pattern from the skill

evals.prompt: Our compliance team says we need better governance for our Salesforce deployments. We have some repos that are SOX-compliant and need extra controls, and we're starting to build Agentforce agents which need security scanning for prompt injections. Can GitHub help with this? We have GitHub Enterprise.
evals.expected_output: Implementation using GitHub Custom Properties for compliance tiers and agentforce-enabled flags, with conditional workflows that enforce SOX compliance checks and Agentforce-specific security scanning (prompt injection detection, grounding validation, PII checks).

assertions.name: Uses GitHub Custom Properties
assertions.description: Solution should leverage Custom Properties for compliance-tier and agentforce-enabled

assertions.name: Implements conditional workflows based on properties
assertions.description: Workflows should have conditional steps based on repository custom properties

assertions.name: Includes SOX compliance checks
assertions.description: Should implement enhanced controls for SOX-compliant repositories

assertions.name: Includes Agentforce security scanning
assertions.description: Should scan for prompt injection vulnerabilities in Agentforce code

assertions.name: Checks for PII exposure in agents
assertions.description: Should validate that agent metadata doesn't contain PII

assertions.name: Validates LLM grounding implementation
assertions.description: Should check for proper grounding methods in Agentforce agents

assertions.name: Explains GitHub Enterprise governance features
assertions.description: Documentation should explain how GitHub Enterprise provides governance capabilities

evals.prompt: Our production deployments take 45 minutes because we have to run all the Apex tests again even though we already validated them on the PR. Is there a way to speed this up?
evals.expected_output: Quick Deploy pattern using GitHub Artifacts to pass validation job IDs between workflows, with explanation of how this reduces deployment time from 45 minutes to ~3 minutes by reusing validation results.

assertions.name: Uses GitHub Artifacts for job ID passing
assertions.description: Should use actions/upload-artifact and actions/download-artifact

assertions.name: Captures validation job ID
assertions.description: PR validation workflow should save the Salesforce validation job ID

assertions.name: Uses sf project deploy quick command
assertions.description: Production deployment should use 'sf project deploy quick --job-id'

assertions.name: Explains time savings (45 min to 3-5 min)
assertions.description: Documentation should highlight the dramatic time reduction

assertions.name: Includes fallback to full deployment
assertions.description: Should handle cases where artifact is missing or expired

assertions.name: References Pattern 3 from skill
assertions.description: Should demonstrate the Quick Deploy pattern from the skill

evals.prompt: I'm running an ALM workshop next week for our Salesforce team. They're used to clicking buttons to deploy and they're nervous about the move to GitHub Actions. What are the key talking points I should cover to show them why this is better? Especially for our move toward Agentforce.
evals.expected_output: Workshop facilitation guidance covering all five GitHub Actions patterns with business value propositions, concrete time/cost savings examples, comparison to declarative tools (without naming specific vendors), and emphasis on Agentforce readiness and multi-cloud scaling. Should include talking points about increased visibility, better governance, and modern orchestration.

assertions.name: Covers all five GitHub Actions patterns
assertions.description: Should discuss Matrix Strategy, OIDC, Quick Deploy, Repository Dispatch, and Custom Properties

assertions.name: Addresses team nervousness about change
assertions.description: Should acknowledge concerns and provide reassurance about the transition

assertions.name: Explains Agentforce-specific benefits
assertions.description: Should highlight why modern ALM is essential for Agentforce (multi-cloud, rapid iteration, security)

assertions.name: Provides concrete time/cost savings examples
assertions.description: Should include specific metrics demonstrating ROI

assertions.name: Avoids naming specific vendor tools
assertions.description: Should not mention Copado or other specific declarative tools by name

assertions.name: Emphasizes API-first orchestration
assertions.description: Should frame GitHub Actions as modern, API-first approach vs button-clicking

assertions.name: Includes workshop structure or agenda
assertions.description: Should provide a structured approach for conducting the workshop
